rationale.R1.1: The contribution claim ('meaningful contribution … simple architectural refinements') is weakly framed; the gap statement relative to prior work is implicit rather than explicit. Improving the draft centrally includes sharpening the thesis and contribution positioning.
rationale.R1.2: The draft makes comparative claims (28.7 vs 27.1 on WMT-2018, exceeding the Google production system) that require internal cross-check. Vaswani et al. originally reported on WMT-2014 EN-DE, not WMT-2018, creating a potential mismatch between the cited baseline and the evaluation setting.
rationale.R1.3: The assertion that TransNova 'exceeds the score reported by the Google production system at the time of publication' is load-bearing for novelty yet unsupported in the presented text; no table, figure, or citation backs it. Improving the draft requires flagging or resolving this evidence gap.
rationale.R1.6: 'Improve this draft' most directly entails prose and paragraph quality. The final paragraph is run-on and conflates results, positioning, and appendix logistics; restructuring it at the sentence/paragraph level is the most unambiguous sub-task in the delegation.
rationale.R1.7: Two references are invoked without bibliographic precision: 'Vaswani et al. baseline transformer' (year/venue unspecified) and 'Google production system at the time of publication' (no citation at all). Improving the draft should ensure these are accurate and properly attributed.

parsed.clarification_question: Should the improvement include verifying and correcting substantive claims — such as the BLEU comparison baseline and the Google production-system assertion — or is the scope limited to prose quality and sentence-level writing polish?
